channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: Israel vows to continue fighting Hezbollah but agrees to talks with Lebanon
item.description: Israel's prime minister said his government would begin talks with Lebanon but vowed to continue attacks against the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, despite a fragile Iran ceasefire.

item.title: How an ancient resin traded for centuries got snarled up by the Iran war
item.description: It's not just energy supplies that have been disrupted by the Iran war. It's also hitting frankincense, a commodity that's been defining trade routes in the Middle East for thousands of years.

item.title: Saudi Arabia says operational activities halted at several energy sites
item.description: Attacks on energy facilities cause a reduced supply and slow recovery, a Saudi official tells state news agency.

item.title: Iran: Strait of Hormuz open with restrictions
item.description: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister says the Strait of Hormuz is open but ships must coordinate with Iranian forces.

item.title: Khamenei says Tehran ‘astonished the world’ during US-Israeli war on Iran
item.description: In a statement read out on television, Mojtaba Khamenei said Tehran will 'demand compensation' for war damages.

item.title: Gaza journalists mourn Al Jazeera’s Wishah, killed by Israel
item.description: Wishah is latest Al Jazeera journalist to be killed by Israel in Gaza since the start of the war in October 2023.

item.title: Netanyahu: ‘No ceasefire in Lebanon’, Israel open to peace talks
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu emphasised that a ceasefire is not in effect in Lebanon

item.title: ARK Invest is betting on underdog drone delivery company Manna to beat out Alphabet and Zipline
item.description: Manna is leaning into a no-frills, ultra-low-cost strategy as regulators open the door to scaled autonomous delivery.

item.title: BDC Bust Creates Bond Opportunity for MFS Investment Management
item.description: The retail exodus from business development companies has dragged the vehicles’ debt to levels that are starting to look attractive, according to MFS Investment Management’s Alex Mackey.

item.title: Bond Traders Hedge Against More Losses as Inflation Data Looms
item.description: Investors in the $31 trillion Treasury market are going into a closely-watched report on consumer prices hedging against more losses in government bonds, as a fragile truce between the US and Iran takes hold.

item.title: Barclays Sounds Out Investors for a Shutterfly Refinancing Deal
item.description: Barclays PLC is sounding out potential investors to help Shutterfly push out its looming debt pile, after talks with private credit lenders lost momentum.

item.title: Software Slumps Anew With Growth Hopes ‘Dashed on the Rocks’
item.description: Shares of software companies fell on Thursday, in the latest example of the group slumping amid concerns about disruption from artificial intelligence services.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Ousts Leonardo CEO, Reappoints Heads of Eni, Enel
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni ousted the chief executive officer of Leonardo SpA, the state-backed aerospace and defense contractor, while retaining CEOs at energy firms Eni SpA and Enel SpA.

item.title: Saudi Oil Output Capacity, Pipeline Flows Cut in Supply Risk
item.description: Saudi Arabia has lost more than half a million barrels a day of oil output capacity because of Iranian attacks while a key pipeline that bypasses the Strait of Hormuz was also hit, ratcheting up risks to global energy supplies from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Stocks Rebound on Hopes Ceasefire Deal Holds | Closing Bell
item.description: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: US Stocks Post Seventh Day of Gains Ahead of US-Iran Talks
item.description: US stocks reversed early morning losses to finish the session in the green as the US and Iran prepared for peace talks in Pakistan, with a fragile ceasefire largely holding.

item.title: Melania Trump says she did not have relationship with Jeffrey Epstein
item.description:
First lady calls on Congress to hold hearing with survivors of late financier’s abuse in statement delivered at White House

  • US politics – live updates

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Melania Trump, the first lady, told reporters on Thursday that she “never had a relationship” with the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and his accomplice Ghislaine Maxwell.

It was unclear which specific accusations spurred the first lady to respond publicly. She delivered her scripted remarks at a podium in the same room Donald Trump used to address the nation on the war in Iran last week.

Continue reading...

item.title: DNC rejects resolution condemning influence of pro-Israel Aipac lobby
item.description:
Panel’s decision a blow to progressives who are unhappy with group’s involvement in Democratic contests

  • US politics – live updates

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

When it comes to addressing how the party should deal with Israel, the Democratic establishment went to their favorite answer: no thanks.

The Democratic National Committee’s resolutions committee voted on Thursday to kill a measure targeting the pro-Israel lobby group Aipac and deferred two further resolutions on Middle East policy to a working group that critics say exists mainly to avoid difficult decisions.

Continue reading...

item.title: US agency proposes rolling back rules for safe disposal of toxic coal ash
item.description:
Advocates warn changes could increase risks of pollutants contaminating water and exposure to toxic waste

The Environmental Protection Agency on Thursday proposed weakened rules governing the safe disposal of ash produced by burning coal. Those regulations were strengthened under the Biden administration as part of a wider crackdown on pollution from coal-fired power plants.

The Trump administration proposed easing standards for monitoring and protecting groundwater near some coal ash sites, rolling back rules forcing the cleanup of entire coal properties instead of just places where ash was dumped. The revisions would also make it easier to reuse coal ash for other purposes.

Continue reading...

item.title: Vladimir Putin announces Orthodox Easter ceasefire with Ukraine
item.description:
Kremlin proposes 32-hour ceasefire starting on Saturday afternoon – with Ukraine expected to agree to plan

Vladimir Putin has declared a 32-hour ceasefire in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter weekend, after an earlier call from Volodymyr Zelenskyy for a pause.

The president’s decree, released by the Kremlin on Thursday, orders Russian forces to observe a ceasefire starting on 4pm Saturday and lasting until the end of Sunday.

Continue reading...
